title: Welcome to the Machine: Client-Server Architecture
description: Understanding the client-server model, the HTTP request/response lifecycle, and what really happens when you hit Enter in a browser.

title: The Ultimate Backend Cheat Sheet & Glossary
description: Interactive technical dictionary establishing fundamental backend vocabulary. Each term links to its deep-dive note.

title: History and Evolution of Backend Systems
description: From CGI scripts to cloud-native serverless — how backend architecture evolved across four decades.

title: The Perfect API Design
description: Core API contracts, design philosophies, and the practical tradeoffs between REST, RPC, and GraphQL.

title: Spring Boot: Enterprise Backend Engineering
description: Master hub for Spring Boot — IoC philosophy, ecosystem map, learning tracks, and links to Core, Data, Web, Security, and Testing pillars.

title: Mastering Spring Core and IoC Containers
description: Deep dive into the Spring ApplicationContext, bean lifecycles, scopes, and clean dependency injection architectures.

title: Spring Boot Auto-Configuration Lifecycle
description: How @SpringBootApplication enables auto-configuration, classpath scanning, and @ConditionalOn* beans under the hood.

title: Properties, Profiles, and YAML Configuration
description: Externalized configuration with application.yml, type-safe @ConfigurationProperties, Spring profiles, and multi-document YAML.

title: JPA, Hibernate, and Spring Data JDBC
description: Spring Data JPA, entity lifecycle states, solving N+1 with fetch strategies, Hibernate proxies, and second-level caching.

title: Declarative Transaction Management in Spring
description: @Transactional mechanics, proxy behavior, propagation (REQUIRED, REQUIRES_NEW), and isolation levels for production data integrity.

title: REST Controllers and Spring MVC
description: @RestController design, request mapping, content negotiation, DTO patterns, path variables vs query parameters.

title: Global Exception Handling in Spring Boot
description: Centralized errors with @ControllerAdvice, @ExceptionHandler, and RFC 7807 Problem Details JSON responses.

title: Spring Security Architecture
description: SecurityFilterChain, DelegatingFilterProxy, stateless JWT authentication, @PreAuthorize, and OAuth2 Resource Server basics.

title: Spring Boot Testing and Slice Annotations
description: Testing pyramid in Spring Boot, @SpringBootTest vs sliced tests, MockMvc, and @MockBean patterns.

title: Spring Boot Actuator and Metrics
description: Securing Actuator endpoints, Micrometer Prometheus integration, and Java 21+ virtual threads configuration.

title: Monoliths vs Microservices
description: A rigorous comparison of monolithic and microservice architectures — when each is right, and the cost of each transition.

title: Modular Monoliths
description: The architectural middle ground — a single deployable with strict internal module boundaries that enable future extraction.

title: Clean Architecture
description: Robert C. Martin's Clean Architecture — dependency rules, layer responsibilities, and how to apply it in a Spring Boot or FastAPI service.

title: Sync vs Async Communication
description: Synchronous blocking calls vs asynchronous non-blocking patterns — thread models, concurrency, and when to use each.

title: gRPC Deep Dive
description: How gRPC works under the hood — Protobuf encoding, HTTP/2 framing, streaming modes, and when to use gRPC over REST.

title: Serialization Protocols
description: JSON, Protocol Buffers, and Apache Avro — encoding formats, tradeoffs, and when to use each in backend systems.

title: The Three Pillars of Observability
description: Metrics, Logs, and Traces — the observability triad that makes production systems debuggable and resilient.

title: Authentication & Authorization
description: JWT, OAuth2, session management — the difference between AuthN and AuthZ, and how to implement them correctly.

title: Rate Limiting & CORS
description: Protecting APIs with rate limiting strategies and understanding Cross-Origin Resource Sharing (CORS) security boundaries.
